evidence: The paper calculates XGBoost feature importance and interprets both magnitude and ranking: "The boiling points followed by vapor pressure showed high scores of 460 and 300, respectively" while m/z values showed unclear patterns. Corroboration is limited to an RI-excluding ablation model showing performance degradation (RMSEs of 0.060, 63, 45 etc.) and domain-knowledge validation that "boiling point and vapor pressure showed a direct relationship with GC retention times." No permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or held-out predictive validation methods are employed. The main conclusions emphasize prediction accuracy rather than variable importance ranking, but the importance findings assert RI is far more influential than m/z without formal statistical substantiation beyond conditional exclusion testing.
